ideation: This sequence simulates the act of throwing a softball.  The animation will focus on the right arm, with the left arm providing balance.  The movement will be smooth but decisive, reflecting the controlled power needed for a good throw.  I will reference existing examples to ensure the arm movements are within the robot's capabilities. Frame 1 will show the robot holding the softball (represented by the hand position) at shoulder height, ready to throw. Frame 2 will show the arm beginning its backswing, with the hand moving slightly behind the robot's body. Frame 3 will be the peak of the backswing, the arm extended and angled backward. Frame 4 shows the arm starting its forward motion, and Frame 5 shows the ball released (represented by hand position).

frames.beat: arms rest, right hand holds ball at shoulder
right arm begins backswing, preparing to throw
right arm reaches back, coiled for power
the throw begins, arm swings forward with force
ball released, right arm follows through
